---
title: "Newtonian Mechanics"
---

## Newton's second law

$$\mathbf{F} = m\,\ddot{\mathbf{r}}$$

For a simple pendulum of length $L$ and mass $m$ (small-angle approximation):

$$\ddot{\theta} = -\frac{g}{L}\,\theta$$

whose solution is simple harmonic motion with angular frequency
$\omega_0 = \sqrt{g/L}$.

## Pendulum animation (Manim)

The animation below was produced with [Manim](https://www.manim.community/) and shows
a simple pendulum swinging back and forth.

```{=html}
<video controls width="720" style="display:block;margin:auto;">
  <source src="../assets/videos/pendulum.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  Your browser does not support the video tag.
</video>
```

> **To regenerate the video** run `./scripts/build_manim.sh` from the repo root.
> The rendered file is saved to `assets/videos/pendulum.mp4`.
